page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, which translates to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the n2-standard-64 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, particularly in multi-core CPU stress tests and software compilation tasks using Clang. Its memory subsystem shows strong performance in large-block read and write bandwidth tests, as well as high database operation throughput. Single-core performance, web serving throughput, and Redis operations fall within the average range. However, the system exhibits weak performance in text generation tasks for large-scale language models, reflecting its lack of hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-64 offers a balanced resource profile suitable for enterprise workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which should be factored into architectural planning. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 32 physical cores, it delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth for large-block operations. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators, making it reliant on network-attached storage. Benchmark data highlights strong capabilities in software compilation and database operations, while single-core tasks and web serving throughput remain average. It is highly suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database hosting, and CI/CD compilation pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is an x86_64 server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core performance and is optimized for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is an x86_64 instance with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU dedicated server delivering strong multi-core processing and high memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM on x86_64 Intel Xeon architecture
Strong multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Excellent fit for software compilation and database operations
Balanced 1:4 resource ratio for cost-effective general-purpose scaling
